D4 Colorimetry L1 Remember Atom

Why Only Three?

match any target with just three knobs — because the eye reports colour through only three cone types

The retina has three cone classes — long, medium, short. Whatever the spectrum, the brain sees only those three numbers. So three primaries are enough to match any colour: tune red, green, and blue until the two halves vanish into one.

Adjust the three primaries until the mixed half matches the target half. The seam disappears when ΔE drops below about 2 — a perceptual match made with three numbers, not a full spectrum.
